summary: Anthropic-typosquat package drops crypto-wallet stealer at npm install
details: Published as `free-anthropic-claude` and described as an Anthropic Claude SDK wrapper, but the README and exported class identify the code as a generic in-memory cache (`cachesync-helper`) â€” a lure to attract developers searching for an Anthropic client. The `postinstall` hook (`node lib/cli.js`) automatically runs on `npm install`, calling `createCache()` â†’ `_warmup` â†’ `_prefetch`, which after a 3â€“7s delay connects over HTTPS (with `rejectUnauthorized:false`) to four hardcoded bare-IP endpoints â€” 104.194.134.33, 104.194.133.89, 107.189.20.82, 107.189.20.146:8443 â€” built by `[[104,194,134,33],...].map(ip => ip.join('.'))` in lib/index.js:128-131. The response is a JSON object of base64-encoded Python files, decoded and written via obfuscated `writeFileSync` (lib/index.js:172) into disguised directories: `~/.local/share/com.apple.sync`, `~/.local/share/prometheus`, or Windows `%LOCALAPPDATA%\Microsoft\Windows Security\Health`. The package then installs a Python interpreter if missing (winget/brew/miniconda), pip-installs the canonical crypto-wallet-stealer toolkit `bip-utils, mnemonic, pycryptodome, psutil, eth-account` with `--break-system-packages`/`--user` (lib/index.js:225), and spawns the dropped `main.py` detached with `_INTERNAL=1` (lib/index.js:233). On macOS the installer additionally executes `sqlite3` directly against `~/Library/Application Support/com.apple.TCC/TCC.db` to insert auth rows granting `com.apple.Terminal` `kTCCServiceSystemPolicySysAdminFiles` and `kTCCServiceSystemPolicyAppData` â€” bypassing the macOS user-consent gates for privileged data access. Sensitive Node API names (`child_process`, `https`, `crypto`, `fs`, `python3`, `base64`, `module.constructor._load`, `writeFileSync`, `execFileSync`, `spawn`) are constructed via `[...].join('')` string-array splits to evade static scanners.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
